articles.title: Harvard Engineers Make a Chip That Can Twist Light
articles.summary: Scientists at Harvard University have built a tiny chip that can twist and control light in real time. This chip uses two special layers that can rotate to change how light behaves. Light can spin in two ways, like left or right hands, and this chip can tell the difference and adjust it. This is important because in science, especially in medicine, light and molecules can act differently based on their twist. For example, some medicines work well in one twist but cause problems in another. The new chip helps scientists study these differences better. It could be used in things like sensing, communication, and quantum technology. The team made it using a small mechanical system to move the layers, making it easy to control without changing parts.

articles.title: Scientists Find a Hidden World in Light
articles.summary: Scientists in South Africa and China have discovered something amazing in light. They found hidden patterns in light that can hold information, like a secret code. These patterns are called topologies and can be very big, up to 48 dimensions, which is like having 48 different directions to store things. They used a tool called entangled photons, which are tiny particles of light that are connected. This discovery could help make computers and other devices work better by protecting information from mistakes. The best part is that many labs already have the equipment to see these patterns, so it's easy for others to use. This finding was published in a science journal and shows that light has more secrets than we thought.

articles.title: A New Plan for Nuclear Power in America
articles.summary: This article talks about how the government is changing the rules for nuclear power in America. A young lawyer named Seth Cohen, who works for the Department of Energy, led a meeting at a big lab in Idaho. He said that the Nuclear Regulatory Commission, which makes sure nuclear plants are safe, will do what the government tells it to do. Some people at the meeting were worried about safety, especially for babies and pregnant women, but Cohen did not seem to care much. The Trump administration wants to build more nuclear power plants to help with artificial intelligence, so they are making new rules quickly. Silicon Valley companies, which have a lot of money, are helping with this plan. This is making some experts nervous because they think safety is very important.

articles.title: A Fast Crocodile Relative from Long Ago
articles.summary: Scientists found a new type of crocodile relative that lived 215 million years ago in Gloucester, UK. Unlike today's crocodiles, this animal lived on land and could run fast like a greyhound. It had long, skinny legs and a light body to move quickly through plants, hunting small animals like reptiles and amphibians. The area was hot and dry with high ground. The species is named after a knight from stories and a teacher who inspired the lead scientist. Fossils were found in old cave deposits in Wales and England, where dead animals washed underground and got buried. This discovery helps us learn about life before dinosaurs.

articles.title: Earth's Moving Plates: A Story from Long Ago
articles.summary: Earth has big pieces called tectonic plates that move and change the land and oceans. Scientists wanted to know when these plates started moving. A new study from Harvard University found the oldest proof that plates were moving 3.5 billion years ago. They looked at very old rocks in Australia that are from a time when tiny life first appeared. By studying tiny magnetic signals in the rocks, like a GPS from the past, they could see how the rocks moved. This shows that Earth was already active long ago, helping shape the planet we live on today.

articles.title: NASA Rover Finds Hidden River Under Mars
articles.summary: NASA's Perseverance rover is exploring Mars to look for signs of ancient life. It landed in Jezero Crater, which used to be a lake. The rover has a special radar called RIMFAX that can see underground. Recently, it discovered an old river delta buried deep under the surface. A river delta is a fan-shaped area where a river drops sand and rocks into a lake. This hidden delta might have been a good place for tiny life forms, called microbes, to live long ago. Scientists are excited because finding water signs on Mars helps us learn if life ever existed there. The rover keeps driving and using its radar to study the rocks and layers underground, giving us new clues about Mars' wet past.
